data.narrative:
eleQtron is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Jan Leisse that has raised €50M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises a sparse comp universe of 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms, including stage-adjacent peers IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to eleQtron. The analysis identified 7 total quantum computing peers but insufficient clustering within narrow filters: zero same-stage comparables, one adjacent-stage company, and zero similar-valuation-tier matches. The threshold for robust peer-median triangulation requires a minimum of three datapoints within aligned cohorts. Relying on all-sector medians would introduce excessive dispersion and mask stage-specific capital efficiency metrics relevant to Series A positioning.

The thinness of the quantum computing comp set, particularly at Series A, prevents traditional multiples-based valuation. Further analysis would require either widening aperture to adjacent deep-tech hardware stages (semiconductors, advanced materials) at the cost of sector-specificity, or employing alternative methods such as discounted cash flow analysis contingent on technology risk assumptions, or venture capital method backed by explicit exit scenario modeling. Valuation of eleQtron requires explicit articulation of technical development milestones, commercialization timeline, and capital intensity assumptions—dimensions not captured in comparable-company analysis alone.
